name: Stir-up-a-Stew
recipeIngredient: OR 1 1/2 lb Pork stew meat, 1-inch cubes
OR 1 1/2 lb Lamb stew meat, 1-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
- SELECT ONE BROTH
- 1 cup Beef broth (13 3/4 oz)
- OR 1 cup Chicken broth (13 3/4 oz)
- OR 1 tablespoon Instant beef bouillon granules + 1 1/2 cups water
- OR 1 tablespoon Instant chicken bouillon granules + 1 1/2 cups water
- SELECT ONE LIQUID SEASONING
- 1 tablespoon Worcestershire sauce
- OR 1 teaspoon Prepared horseradish
- OR 1 tablespoon Soy sauce
- OR 1 teaspoon Prepared mustard
- SELECT ONE DRY SEASONING
- 1 teaspoon Dried Thyme, crushed
- OR 1 teaspoon Dried Marjoram, crushed
- OR 1 teaspoon Dried Basil, crushed
- OR 1 teaspoon Dried Oregano, crushed
- SELECT VEGETABLE (S)
- 5 cups Chopped, sliced, or cubed vegetables (carrots, celery, green peppers, onions, parsnips, potatoes, rutabagas, or parsnips)
- USE ALL OF THESE
- 1/4 cup All-purpose flour
- 1 Bay leaf
- 3/4 teaspoon Salt
- 1/4 teaspoon Pepper
1. In a plastic bag, combine the flour, salt, and pepper. Add half of the desired meat at a time to the bag, shaking to coat well.
2. In a Dutch oven, cook the meat in hot oil until lightly browned. Drain excess fat. Return the meat to the pan.
3. Add the selected broth, liquid seasoning, dry seasoning, bay leaf, and salt to the Dutch oven. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer, covered, for beef (1 hour), pork (1 hour), or lamb (1 hour); chicken (30 minutes).
4. Add 5 cups of the selected vegetables. Cover and simmer until the vegetables are tender, about 30 minutes.
5. Discard the bay leaf. Return any cooked chicken to the stew.
